title: Get started with DStressR
text: DStressR expects long measurement table one row per measured promoter-compound-replicate observation. complete rectangular screen, row count approximately: table may contain additional rows screen repeated across batches, library plates, measurement plates, experimental days. important point technical covariates remain table can modeled rather accidentally averaged away. required biological technical information : promoter reporter construct compound library-well identifier negative-control compound, usually DMSO luminescence summary growth summary replicate optional technical covariates batch plate Column names fixed. mapped explicitly prepare_assay(). original Campylobacter promoter-library workflow, convenience helper read_campylobacter_expression() joins two exported files: expression_values.tsv.gz: one row per promoter-library-well-replicate observation. must contain either srn_code libplate well, plus promoter, luminescence, growth, technical columns used downstream. LibMap.txt: one row per compound/library well columns Library plate, Well, ProductName, Catalog Number. helper reconstructs library-well key paste0("lp", Library plate, "_", Well) LibMap.txt, joins compound annotations onto expression table, returns long table number rows expression_values.tsv.gz.
code: n_promoters x (n_compound_wells + n_control_wells) x n_replicates expression_df <- read_campylobacter_expression(   expression_file = "expression_values.tsv.gz",   libmap_file = "LibMap.txt" )  assay <- prepare_assay(   expression_df,   promoter = "promoter",   compound = "srn_code",   control = "DMSO",   lux = "LUX.AUC_16",   growth = "od_16h.measured",   batch = "batch",   plate = "libplate",   replicate = "replicate" )

title: Get started with DStressR
text: fit_destress() fits promoter compound effects accounting technical covariates. result table reports DMSO-relative total effect promoter-specific effect subtracting compound-wide effect. key columns : total_effect: DMSO-relative response promoter-compound pair. global_effect: average compound-wide response across promoters. specific_effect: promoter-specific deviation compound-wide effect. specific_pvalue specific_padj: test BH-adjusted p-value. direct fitting functions remain available existing scripts, fit_destress() recommended entry point new model-based analyses.

title: Get started with DStressR
text: screen contains matched background reporter, Empty Vector Control, background reporter can used response construction. background_promoter supplied, DStressR uses Huber calibration default; least-squares calibration direct subtraction remain available background_method.

title: E. coli promoter-compound screen
text: package application, wild-type reporter data filtered rows passed original quality-control flag. Water used reference condition. Empty Vector Control reporter supplied prepare_assay() matched background reporter, default response model performs Huber-calibrated background adjustment model-based testing. full comparison workflow kept outside package vignette build creates manuscript-scale figures tables. repository root, run: workflow compares three hit sets: reconstructed Binsfeld-style Wilcoxon/Z-score reference rule DStressR default modeled response DStressR default modeled response EVC-Huber calibration current wild-type comparison gives: Additional scripts analysis/ecoli_promoter_screen/ recreate two-method report, volcano plots, heatmaps, Venn diagrams, response-modeling figures used manuscript development. Generated outputs written analysis/outputs/ intentionally ignored Git.
code: wt_auc <- subset(   binsfeld_reporter_auc,   strain == "WT" & removed == "No" )  assay <- prepare_assay(   wt_auc,   promoter = "promoter",   compound = "compound",   control = "Water",   lux = "lux_auc",   growth = "od_auc",   growth_exponent = "estimate",   batch = "dose_level",   replicate = "replicate",   growth_covariates = "replicate",   numeric_covariates = "dose_level",   background_promoter = "EVC",   background_by = c("compound", "dose_level", "replicate") )  fit <- fit_destress(   assay,   preset = "model",   technical = c("replicate", "dose_level"),   empirical_bayes = TRUE,   adjustment = "by_promoter",   interaction = FALSE )  hits <- call_hits(   results(fit),   fdr = 0.05,   effect = "specific_effect" ) Rscript analysis/ecoli_promoter_screen/run_evc_calibrated_analysis.R Reference WT hits: 53 DStressR default modeled-response WT hits: 80 DStressR EVC-Huber WT hits: 92 All three analyses: 35 Reference-only hits: 16 DStressR modeled-only hits: 9 DStressR EVC-Huber-only hits: 19 Modeled + EVC-Huber only: 36 Union significant by at least one primary analysis: 117

title: Original median-polish workflow
text: optional normality_results table corresponds original DMSO normality check: Shapiro-Wilk Lilliefors p-values computed pre-polish DMSO-centered fold changes within promoter-libplate-replicate group, BH-adjusted across groups. replicate-level table corresponds original expression_df.pvalues.tsv.gz output: pair-level table corresponds original hit-calling table: DMSO excluded controls removed, largest replicate-level p-value retained promoter-compound pair, BH correction applied within promoter.
code: library(DStressR)  expression_df <- read_campylobacter_expression(   expression_file = "expression_values.tsv.gz",   libmap_file = "LibMap.txt" )  libmap <- read.delim("LibMap.txt", check.names = FALSE) libmap$srn_code <- paste0("lp", libmap[["Library plate"]], "_", libmap[["Well"]])  dmso_srn_codes <- libmap$srn_code[libmap$ProductName == "DMSO"] dmso_noisy_srn_codes <- libmap$srn_code[libmap$ProductName == "DMSO noisy"]  legacy <- fit_workflow(   expression_df,   workflow = "median_polish",   promoter = "promoter",   compound = "srn_code",   libplate = "libplate",   replicate = "replicate",   response = "log2.auc.16hmeasured.normed",   control = dmso_srn_codes,   exclude = dmso_noisy_srn_codes,   normality = TRUE,   maxiter = 1000,   eps = 1e-8 ) replicate_pvalues <- legacy$replicate_results  head(replicate_pvalues) hit_table <- legacy$pair_results  head(hit_table) table(hit_table$hit)

title: Original median-polish workflow
text: fit_workflow(..., workflow = "median_polish") follows original hit-determination steps: Build promoter_libplate_replicate = paste(promoter, libplate, replicate, sep = "_"). Compute DMSO mean normalized response within promoter-libplate-replicate group. Compute log2FC = normalized.lux - avg.dmso.expression. Form matrix promoter-libplate-replicate groups rows srn_code compound/library wells columns. Apply stats::medpolish(..., na.rm = TRUE, maxiter = 1000, eps = 1e-8). Use median-polish residuals log2FC.polished. Estimate DMSO residual mean standard deviation within promoter-libplate-replicate group. Compute zscore = (log2FC.polished - dmso.avg_dmsoFC) / dmso.stdv_dmsoFC. Compute two-sided Gaussian p-values. hit calling, remove DMSO/noisy-DMSO wells, keep largest p-value across replicates promoter-compound pair, apply BH correction within promoter. normality = TRUE, function also runs workflow’s DMSO normality screen median polishing. Lilliefors test uses nortest::lillie.test(); install nortest set normality_methods = "shapiro" Shapiro-Wilk check needed.

title: Salmonella Empty Vector workflow
text: replicate-level table contains EVC-normalized fold change, DMSO null parameters, z-scores, raw p-values: pair-level table reproduces original conservative aggregation: promoter-compound pair, retain largest replicate-level p-value apply BH adjustment within promoter.
code: expression_df <- read.delim(gzfile("lux_auc_filtered_median.tsv.gz"),                             check.names = FALSE) libmap <- read.delim(gzfile("LibMap.tsv.gz"), check.names = FALSE)  libmap$libplate <- sub("LibPlate", "lp", libmap[["Library plate"]]) libmap$srn_code <- paste(libmap$libplate, libmap[["New well"]], sep = "_")  dmso_srn_codes <- libmap$srn_code[libmap[["Catalog Number"]] == "DMSO"] dmso_noisy_srn_codes <- libmap$srn_code[libmap[["Catalog Number"]] == "DMSO noisy"]  evc <- fit_workflow(   expression_df,   workflow = "empty_vector_control",   promoter = "promoter",   compound = "srn_code",   replicate = "replicate",   response = "log2.lux.normed.centered",   empty_vector_promoter = "PEVC3",   control = dmso_srn_codes,   exclude = dmso_noisy_srn_codes,   remove_promoters = "PmgrR" ) replicate_pvalues <- evc$replicate_results  head(replicate_pvalues) hit_table <- evc$pair_results  head(hit_table) table(hit_table$hit)

title: Salmonella Empty Vector workflow
text: fit_workflow(..., workflow = "empty_vector_control") follows original Salmonella hit-determination logic: Remove noisy DMSO wells failed reporters, example PmgrR. Keep already centered response column, usually log2.lux.normed.centered. srn_code, compute average PEVC3 response across PEVC3 replicates. Compute log.evcfc = expression_value - average_PEVC3_expression_for_srn_code. Remove PEVC3 reference rows tested reporter table. Gather DMSO log.evcfc values within promoter-replicate group. Estimate dmso.mean dmso.stdv. Compute zscore = (log.evcfc - dmso.mean) / dmso.stdv. Compute two-sided Gaussian p-values. Retain largest replicate-level p-value promoter-compound pair. Apply BH correction within promoter label hits.

title: Differential Stress-Response Modeling for Chemical Genomics Screens
text: repository hosts DStressR R package companion analysis workflow high-throughput bacterial chemical genomics screens. DStressR designed go hand hand DGrowthR: DGrowthR models bacterial growth curves, DStressR models promoter-activity responses accounting growth, compound-wide effects, technical covariates, promoter-specific uncertainty. title: Differential Stress-Response Modeling for Chemical Genomics Screens
text: DStressR exposes statistical analyses staged fit_destress() interface. major choices explicit: normalization, test statistic p-value calculation, replicate aggregation, p-value adjustment. Named presets reproduce established workflows: preset = "model" model-based DStressR analysis preset = "median_polish" original median-polish p-value workflow preset = "empty_vector_control" Empty Vector Control workflow model workflow starts assay prepared prepare_assay():
code: library(DStressR)  screen <- simulate_screen(seed = 1)  assay <- prepare_assay(   screen,   promoter = "promoter",   compound = "compound",   control = "DMSO",   lux = "LUX.AUC_16",   growth = "od_16h.measured",   batch = "batch",   replicate = "replicate" )  fit <- fit_destress(   assay,   preset = "model",   technical = c("batch", "replicate"),   empirical_bayes = TRUE )  tab <- results(fit) hits <- call_hits(tab, fdr = 0.05, effect = "specific_effect")

EVC-Huber workflow overlaps 37 53 reference hits. Equivalently, using staged options directly: Growth-response normalization model path controlled prepare_assay() passing arguments fit_destress(). Use growth_exponent = 1 fixed log2(LUX / OD) normalization, growth_exponent = "estimate" estimate promoter-specific alpha_g values controls, pass named promoter vector. model-based analyses, empirical_bayes = TRUE default moderated model empirical_bayes = FALSE ordinary Student-tt sensitivity model. code: data("binsfeld_reporter_auc")  wt_auc <- subset(   binsfeld_reporter_auc,   strain == "WT" & removed == "No" )  assay <- prepare_assay(   wt_auc,   promoter = "promoter",   compound = "compound",   control = "Water",   lux = "lux_auc",   growth = "od_auc",   growth_exponent = "estimate",   batch = "dose_level",   replicate = "replicate",   growth_covariates = "replicate",   numeric_covariates = "dose_level",   background_promoter = "EVC",   background_by = c("compound", "dose_level", "replicate") )  fit <- fit_destress(   assay,   preset = "model",   technical = c("replicate", "dose_level"),   empirical_bayes = TRUE,   adjustment = "by_promoter",   interaction = FALSE ) fit <- fit_destress(   screen,   normalization = "model",   testing = "moderated_t",   aggregation = "none",   adjustment = "global",   promoter = "promoter",   compound = "compound",   control = "DMSO",   lux = "LUX.AUC_16",   growth = "od_16h.measured",   growth_exponent = "estimate",   batch = "batch",   replicate = "replicate",   technical = c("batch", "replicate") )

Required columns : Library plate Well ProductName Catalog Number helper converts compound key srn_code = paste0("lp", Library plate, "_", Well) joins ProductName Catalog Number onto expression table. returned object number rows expression_values.tsv.gz, compound annotations added. joined table can passed directly prepare_assay(). public template script scripts/export_campy_default_model_template.R records exact Campylobacter default-model call used regenerate local package output analysis/outputs/package_results/destress_moderated_pair_results.tsv proprietary expression table. sets empirical_bayes = TRUE, interaction = FALSE, background_rank = 0, uses promoter-specific estimated growth exponents, exports specific_* result columns expected downstream analysis scripts. scripts/README.md file highlights data-free export template canonical place inspect actual manuscript model call. reproduce original median-polish workflow, provide DMSO library-well IDs optional noisy-DMSO well IDs LibMap.txt:
code: expression_df <- read_campylobacter_expression(   expression_file = "expression_values.tsv.gz",   libmap_file = "LibMap.txt" ) libmap <- read.delim("LibMap.txt", check.names = FALSE) libmap$srn_code <- paste0("lp", libmap[["Library plate"]], "_", libmap[["Well"]])  dmso_srn_codes <- libmap$srn_code[libmap$ProductName == "DMSO"] dmso_noisy_srn_codes <- libmap$srn_code[libmap$ProductName == "DMSO noisy"]  legacy <- fit_destress(   expression_df,   preset = "median_polish",   response = "log2.auc.16hmeasured.normed",   control = dmso_srn_codes,   exclude = dmso_noisy_srn_codes,   normality = TRUE )  dmso_normality <- legacy$normality_results replicate_pvalues <- legacy$replicate_results hit_table <- legacy$pair_results

title: Differential Stress-Response Modeling for Chemical Genomics Screens
text: Salmonella workflow uses stronger control design DMSO-normalization. addition DMSO wells, includes Empty Vector Control reporters, especially PEVC3, measure compound-specific background Lux signal without promoter insert. DStressR exposes baseline fit_empty_vector_control(). required processed expression table output Salmonella Lux-estimation step, usually lux_auc_filtered_median.tsv.gz. long table one row per promoter-library-well-replicate observation contains: promoter srn_code replicate log2.lux.normed.centered library map LibMap.tsv.gz, columns: Library plate New well Catalog Number ProductName local Salmonella workflow output, DStressR implementation recovers original hit_table.tsv.gz numerically, including final hit labels.
code: expression_df <- read.delim(gzfile("lux_auc_filtered_median.tsv.gz"),                             check.names = FALSE) libmap <- read.delim(gzfile("LibMap.tsv.gz"), check.names = FALSE)  libmap$libplate <- sub("LibPlate", "lp", libmap[["Library plate"]]) libmap$srn_code <- paste(libmap$libplate, libmap[["New well"]], sep = "_")  dmso_srn_codes <- libmap$srn_code[libmap[["Catalog Number"]] == "DMSO"] dmso_noisy_srn_codes <- libmap$srn_code[libmap[["Catalog Number"]] == "DMSO noisy"]  evc <- fit_destress(   expression_df,   preset = "empty_vector_control",   response = "log2.lux.normed.centered",   empty_vector_promoter = "PEVC3",   control = dmso_srn_codes,   exclude = dmso_noisy_srn_codes,   remove_promoters = "PmgrR" )  replicate_pvalues <- evc$replicate_results hit_table <- evc$pair_results

text: function implements Salmonella StressRegNet workflow promoter activity normalized Empty Vector Control (EVC) reporter measured compound/library well. starts long expression table, subtracts compound-specific EVC average promoter-replicate value, estimates promoter-replicate DMSO null distributions, applies original conservative replicate aggregation.

title: Reproduce Empty Vector Control normalization — fit_empty_vector_control
text: data Long expression table one row per promoter-compound-replicate observation. promoter, compound, replicate Column names identifying promoter, compound/library well, replicate. response Column containing expression value normalize. Salmonella workflow log2.lux.normed.centered. empty_vector_promoter Promoter/control strain used Empty Vector reference. original Salmonella workflow uses PEVC3. control Character vector compound/library-well IDs used DMSO controls null distribution. exclude Character vector compound/library-well IDs removed normalization hit calling, example noisy DMSO wells. remove_promoters Promoters removed normalization, example failed reporter strains. fdr FDR threshold used assign hit class pair-level table. require_complete_empty_vector TRUE, require EVC replicate values compound finite computing EVC average. matches original workflow's effective behavior two PEVC3 replicates.

what: Reproduce the legacy median-polish workflow — fit_median_polish
title: Reproduce the legacy median-polish workflow — fit_median_polish
text: function implements original median-polish hit-determination workflow used Campylobacter promoter-library screen. starts long expression table, centers promoter-library-plate-replicate group DMSO wells, applies stats::medpolish() resulting promoter-libplate-replicate compound matrix, computes z-test p-values polished DMSO residual distribution.

title: Reproduce the legacy median-polish workflow — fit_median_polish
code: fit_median_polish(   data,   promoter = "promoter",   compound = "srn_code",   libplate = "libplate",   replicate = "replicate",   response = "log2.auc.16hmeasured.normed",   control,   exclude = character(),   fdr = 0.05,   normality = FALSE,   normality_methods = c("shapiro", "lilliefors"),   maxiter = 1000,   eps = 1e-08 )

title: Reproduce the legacy median-polish workflow — fit_median_polish
text: data Long expression table one row per promoter-compound-replicate observation. promoter, compound, libplate, replicate Column names identifying promoter, compound/library well, library plate, replicate. response Column containing already growth-normalized log2 response, example log2.auc.16hmeasured.normed. control Character vector compound/library-well IDs used DMSO controls. exclude Character vector compound/library-well IDs remove median polishing hit calling, example noisy DMSO wells. fdr FDR threshold used assign hit class pair-level table. normality TRUE, test pre-polish DMSO-centered fold changes within promoter-library-plate-replicate group. normality_methods Character vector containing "shapiro" /"lilliefors". Lilliefors test requires suggested nortest package. maxiter, eps Passed stats::medpolish().

title: Reproduce the legacy median-polish workflow — fit_median_polish
text: list class destress_median_polish replicate_results, pair_results, polished_matrix, medpolish, optional normality_results components.

title: Reproduce the legacy median-polish workflow — fit_median_polish
text: promoter-compound hit table follows original conservative replicate aggregation: DMSO excluded control wells removed, largest replicate-level p-value retained promoter-compound pair, p-values BH-adjusted within promoter.

what: Prepare a chemical-genomics assay table — prepare_assay
title: Prepare a chemical-genomics assay table — prepare_assay
text: Computes growth-adjusted log2 promoter-activity response luminescence growth measurements. default, promoter-specific growth exponents estimated control wells available technical-factor adjustment shrunk toward global control-well slope. Set growth_exponent = 1 reproduce current workflow's log2(LUX / OD) response.

title: Prepare a chemical-genomics assay table — prepare_assay
code: prepare_assay(   data,   promoter = "promoter",   compound = "compound",   control = "DMSO",   lux = "lux",   growth = "growth",   growth_exponent = "estimate",   control_values = control,   response = NULL,   batch = NULL,   plate = NULL,   replicate = NULL,   growth_covariates = NULL,   numeric_covariates = NULL,   background_promoter = NULL,   background_method = c("none", "subtract", "lm", "huber"),   background_by = NULL,   pseudocount = 1e-08 )

title: Prepare a chemical-genomics assay table — prepare_assay
text: data data frame one row per promoter-compound-replicate well. promoter, compound Column names identifying promoter compound. control Label compound negative control, usually DMSO. lux, growth Column names luminescence growth summaries. growth_exponent Fixed coefficient growth normalization, named vector keyed promoter, "estimate" estimate promoter-specific exponents controls. control_values Values compound used controls growth exponent estimation. Defaults control. response Optional existing response column. supplied, lux growth used compute response. batch, plate, replicate Optional technical-factor column names. growth_exponent = "estimate", columns also used covariates estimating promoter-specific growth exponents unless growth_covariates supplied. growth_covariates Optional technical covariate column names used estimating promoter-specific growth exponents control wells. NULL, DStressR uses supplied batch, plate, replicate columns backwards compatibility. numeric_covariates Optional subset technical covariate column names remain numeric model matrices. optional covariates converted factors. background_promoter Optional reporter promoter used background reference, e.g. Empty Vector Control. supplied, default background method "huber". background reporter matched promoters background_by, response calibrated, background reporter excluded model-based testing. background_method One "none", "subtract", "lm", "huber". omitted, DStressR uses "none" background_promoter supplied "huber" one supplied. "subtract" subtracts matched background response. "lm" "huber" replace non-background promoter response residuals promoter-wise calibration matched background. background_by Columns used match observation background reporter. Defaults compound plus supplied technical columns. pseudocount Added log2 transformation.
